figures.caption: Fig. 1. Comparison of accuracy and energy consumption on the UAV123 dataset. STATrack achieves 66.9% AUC with only 5.7 mJ inference energy, demonstrating a superior accuracy–energy trade-off.

TABLE III PARAMETER SENSITIVITY ANALYSIS ON FOUR BENCHMARKS. WE INVESTIGATE THE IMPACT OF THE BASE WEIGHT λbase AND THE DYNAMIC ADJUSTMENT AMPLITUDE β . THE BEST RESULTS IN EACH GROUP ARE HIGHLIGHTED IN BOLD.

Fig. 3. Qualitative comparison of STATrack with six state-of-the-art UAV trackers on four video sequences from DTB70, UAV123, VisDrone2018, and UAVDT, namely Animal3, Car7, S1607, and uav0000088 00000 s.

Fig. 4. For each example, we visualize target regions from different frames of the same video sequence (top row), followed by the corresponding feature maps generated by STATrack without AMIM (middle row) and with the proposed AMIM (bottom row).

Fig. 2. Overview of the proposed framework. The architecture consists of an efficient SNN-based Transformer backbone for joint template–search feature learning, followed by a spiking head. (Bottom-left) Illustration of the proposed Adaptive Mutual Information Maximization (AMIM) module. (Bottom-right) Detailed structure of the adaptive dynamic weighting strategy used in AMIM. Note that {Z} denotes a batch of input samples, while {Z′} represents a randomly shuffled batch of Z.

TABLE I COMPARISON OF STATRACK WITH LIGHTWEIGHT TRACKERS IN TERMS OF PRECISION (PREC.), SUCCESS RATE (SUCC.), AND POWER CONSUMPTION (MJ) ON DTB70, UAVDT, VISDRONE2018, AND UAV123. RED, BLUE, AND GREEN INDICATE THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD BEST RESULTS, RESPECTIVELY. NOTE THAT PERCENTAGE SYMBOLS (%) ARE OMITTED FOR BOTH PRECISION AND SUCCESS RATE VALUES.
